title.en: I owe you one! 신세 졌어!

description.en: When you receive great help from a friend! Practice expressing gratitude and a promise to repay with 고마워 and 신세 졌어.
description.ko: 친구에게 큰 도움을 받았을 때! 고마워와 신세 졌어를 사용해 고마운 마음과 보답하겠다는 약속을 전해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Gratitude. Favor. Debt. Friendship. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 어제 제가 급하게 부탁한 자료 정리해 줘서 정말 고마워요.
content.translation: John! Thank you so much for organizing the data I asked for urgently yesterday.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 덕분에 회의 무사히 잘 끝냈어요!
content.translation: Thanks to you. the meeting ended successfully!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm glad I could help. Mina. It wasn't that hard for me anyway.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니에요. 존 아니었으면 밤샐 뻔했다니까요.
content.translation: No. I would have stayed up all night if it wasn't for you.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 고마워. 이번에 제대로 신세 졌어!
content.translation: Thank you so much. I really owe you one this time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Don't mention it! That's what friends are for.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 제가 맛있는 점심 살게요. 꼭이에요!
content.translation: I'll buy you a delicious lunch next time. I mean it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 고마워. This is the first word everyone learns. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 가장 기본적이면서도 가장 중요한 표현이죠.
content.translation: Yes. It's the most basic yet most important expression.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 상대방의 배려나 도움에 마음을 표현할 때 써보세요.
content.translation: Use it when expressing your heart for someone's consideration or help.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 고마워.
content.translation: Thank you.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 신세 졌어 라는 말은 상대방에게 큰 도움이나 은혜를 입었을 때 쓰는 리액션이에요. 영어의 I owe you one 과 느낌이 아주 비슷해요.
content.translation: And the phrase 'Sin-se jyeot-eo' is a reaction used when you've received great help or a favor from someone. It feels very similar to 'I owe you one' in English.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 신세 졌어.
content.translation: I owe you one.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is there a way to say "I'll pay you back".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 다음에 내가 쏠게 라고 해보세요.
content.translation: In that case. try saying 'Da-eum-e nae-ga ssol-ge'.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 내가 밥이나 커피를 사겠다는 아주 한국적인 보답의 표현이에요.
content.translation: It's a very Korean expression of repayment. meaning I'll buy lunch or coffee next time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine I lent you my laptop for a whole week because yours was broken.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 덕분에 이번 주 과제 잘 마쳤어요. 노트북 빌려줘서 정말 고마워.
content.translation: John. thanks to you. I finished my assignment well this week. Thank you so much for lending me your laptop.

content.speaker: John
content.text: No problem. I'm just happy you finished your work on time.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여기요! 깨끗하게 썼어요.
content.translation: Here it is! I used it cleanly.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 이번에 진짜 신세 졌어. 내가 어떻게 보답하면 좋을까?
content.translation: I really owe you one this time. How can I repay you?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Hmm... how about a cup of coffee?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 겨우 커피요? 안 돼요! 오늘 저녁은 제가 쏠게요. 맛있는 거 먹으러 가요!
content.translation: Just coffee? No way! Dinner is on me today. Let's go eat something delicious!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay! I won't say no to that.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Gratitude makes every relationship stronger.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 고마울 땐 미안해하기보다 오늘 배운 표현들로 당당하고 기분 좋게 고마움을 표현해 보세요!
content.translation: Right. Instead of feeling sorry when you're thankful. try expressing your gratitude confidently and pleasantly with the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "thankful" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 고마워!
content.translation: Thank you!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 신세 졌어!
content.translation: I owe you one!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 다음에 내가 쏠게!
content.translation: Dinner is on me next time!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 덕분에 살았어!
content.translation: You saved my life!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 나중에 꼭 갚을게!
content.translation: I'll definitely pay you back!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 주변 사람들에게 고마워라고 말하며 따뜻한 마음을 나누는 하루 되시길 바랄게요.
content.translation: I hope you all have a warm day sharing your heart by saying thank you to those around you today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 이번에 큰 신세를 졌네요. (I've become greatly indebted this time.)
도움에 보답하고 싶어요. (I want to repay you for the help.)
부모님의 은혜에 감사드립니다. (I am grateful for my parents' grace.)
오늘 점심은 제가 쏠게요. (Lunch is on me today.)
어제 밤을 새워서 과제를 끝냈어요. (I stayed up all night yesterday to finish the assignment.)
존의 따뜻한 배려에 감동했어요. (I was moved by John's warm consideration.)
